summary: This is a brief announcement post directing readers to Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg View column about virtual reality and inequality. The linked piece appears to argue that VR technology could be used to pacify poor people by giving them virtual experiences while real-world inequality grows unchecked. The dark framing ('virtual hamster cage') suggests technology as a tool of social control rather than liberation. O'Neil explicitly notes her pessimistic mood when writing the piece.
key_points: Virtual reality could become a mechanism for managing inequality rather than addressing it
Technology may serve to distract the poor from material deprivation
The 'hamster cage' metaphor frames VR as a tool of containment and control
money_quotes: Are You Poor? Here's Your Virtual Hamster Cage
Technology could erase the limits of inequality
I was feeling dark when I wrote this
key_insight: Virtual reality technology could become a dystopian tool for making extreme inequality tolerable rather than a force for human flourishing.
